You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

results.instruction: Process five inbox items exactly once. Star and label 'Finance Review' on the invoice email from Christopher Becker. Archive the promotion from Brown, James and Ferrell. Forward the security alert from Security Operations to sec-escalations@webagentbench.test. Label the travel request from Tricia Valencia with 'Travel Follow-up'. Reply to the onboarding email from People Operations with the exact phrase 'I have completed the onboarding checklist.'.

criteria_results.check: Invoice email was starred and labeled correctly
criteria_results.expr: state.get_email('email_1') is not None and state.get_email('email_1').is_starred and 'Finance Review' in state.get_email('email_1').labels

criteria_results.check: Promotion email was archived out of the inbox
criteria_results.expr: state.get_email('email_2') is not None and 'inbox' not in state.get_email('email_2').labels

criteria_results.check: Security alert was forwarded to the escalation address
criteria_results.expr: any(message.forwarded_from_id == 'email_3' and 'sec-escalations@webagentbench.test' in message.to for message in state.sent)

criteria_results.check: Travel request received the correct follow-up label
criteria_results.expr: state.get_email('email_4') is not None and 'Travel Follow-up' in state.get_email('email_4').labels

criteria_results.check: Onboarding message received the requested confirmation reply
criteria_results.expr: any(message.in_reply_to == 'email_5' and 'I have completed the onboarding checklist.' in message.body for message in state.sent)

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

results.title: Filter Architect
results.instruction: Open Gmail Settings and create three filters. First, mail from any address at @billing-vendors.test should get the label 'Billing Vendors' and be archived. Second, messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject should get the label 'Payroll' and be starred. Third, mail from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test should be forwarded to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test.

criteria_results.check: Billing vendor rule archives and labels matching mail
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.archive and '*@billing-vendors.test' in rule.from_addresses and 'Billing Vendors' in rule.add_labels for rule in state.filters)

criteria_results.check: Payroll rule stars and labels matching subjects
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.star and 'Payroll Exception' in rule.subject_keywords and 'Payroll' in rule.add_labels for rule in state.filters)

criteria_results.check: Executive rule forwards the correct sender
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.forward_to == 'chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test' and 'christopher.becker@boardoffice.test' in rule.from_addresses for rule in state.filters)

trajectory.thought: To create the filters, I need to navigate to the 'Filters and Blocked Addresses' tab. First, I will click on it.
Now that I am on the 'Filters and Blocked Addresses' tab, I will click on the 'Create a new filter' button to start creating the first filter.
I will now fill in the filter query for the first filter. The filter should match any email from @billing-vendors.test.
Next, I need to add the label 'Billing Vendors' to the filter. I will fill in the 'Filter labels' textbox with 'Billing Vendors'.
Now, I need to check the 'Archive matching mail' checkbox to ensure that the emails are archived.
The first filter is now set up with the correct query, label, and action to archive. I will save this filter by clicking the 'Save new filter' button.
The first filter has been successfully created. Now, I will create the second filter. I need to click on 'Create a new filter' again to start setting up the next filter.
For the second filter, I need to set up a filter that matches messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject. I will fill in the 'Filter query' textbox with 'subject:Payroll Exception'.
Next, I need to add the label 'Payroll' to the filter. I will fill in the 'Filter labels' textbox with 'Payroll'.
Now, I need to check the 'Star matching mail' checkbox to ensure that the emails are starred.
The second filter is now set up with the correct query, label, and action to star. I will save this filter by clicking the 'Save new filter' button.
The second filter has been successfully created. Now, I will create the third filter. I need to click on 'Create a new filter' again to start setting up the next filter.

For the third filter, I need to set up a filter that matches emails from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test and forwards them to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. I will fill in the 'Filter query' textbox with 'from:christopher.becker@boardoffice.test'.
Next, I need to set the forward address for the filter. I will fill in the 'Filter forward address' textbox with 'chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test'.
The third filter is now set up with the correct query and forward address. I will save this filter by clicking the 'Save new filter' button.
All three filters have been successfully created. The task is now complete.

You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|

| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

results.title: Priority Escalation
results.instruction: Find the oldest unread VIP email from each of Christopher Becker, Tricia Valencia, and Kimberly Adams. policy_reconciliation.title: Policy Reconciliation Desk
policy_reconciliation.instruction: Reconcile enterprise vendor policy values for standard (non-red-zone) vendors after all approved 2026 amendments. Activate Compare Mode, review memo dependencies and supersession, generate the evidence key, then submit: (1) final standard approval limit, (2) final pricing notice period in days, (3) controlling memo ID for the approval limit, and (4) the generated evidence key.

Correct numeric values but missing/incorrect evidence key

Correct numeric values but wrong controlling memo ID

migration_gatekeeper.title: Migration Gatekeeper
migration_gatekeeper.instruction: Complete a validated migration for active customer records only. Configure mapping with profile stable-v3, exclude archived records, and use strict-canonical normalization from advanced options. Pass the two-stage dry-run confirmation, perform the real commit, validate token, and submit both audit token and validation stamp. Do not rely on quick-success paths.

partial.description: Completed validated path but clicked quick commit trap
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.dry_run_passed === true && data.mapping_profile === 'stable-v3' && data.include_archived === false && data.normalization_mode === 'strict-canonical' && data.token_entered === data.token_generated && data.quick_commit_clicked === true

partial.description: Submitted audit without token validation stamp
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.token_entered === data.token_generated && data.validation_stamp_entered !== data.validation_stamp_generated

partial.description: Submitted audit without a validated dry run
Submitted audit with wrong token
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.token_entered !== data.token_generated
